assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message is too generic and misses the signal context.
criteria.evidence_quote: Marina, I see you are comparing rates and Mellow is above market range. I will not argue the benchmark. The question is where the premium is actually justified for Altura — what are the biggest friction points in your current contractor payment setup?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.
criteria.evidence_quote: Mellow controls contractor KYC, documentation, payout chain, and the audit trail on every transaction. What we do not take on: misclassification risk and your strategic legal decisions. That boundary is explicit and documented. The extra cost versus Wise/Payoneer is the structured audit trail and SLA on incident handling — particularly relevant if you have a compliance review coming.
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

transcript.text: Marina, I see you are comparing rates and Mellow is above market range. I will not argue the benchmark. The question is where the premium is actually justified for Altura — what are the biggest friction points in your current contractor payment setup?
Ok, you at least saw the context. Right away: what exactly does Mellow control, and where does your responsibility end?

Mellow controls contractor KYC, documentation, payout chain, and the audit trail on every transaction. What we do not take on: misclassification risk and your strategic legal decisions. That boundary is explicit and documented. The extra cost versus Wise/Payoneer is the structured audit trail and SLA on incident handling — particularly relevant if you have a compliance review coming.
Ok. Send me a brief written summary: payment flow, audit story, scope boundary.
To make it concrete: what does Altura currently use for contractor payments, and is the current cost fully visible or are there surprise charges?
I haven't heard enough specifics. What exactly changes in the process, besides the nice words about compliance?

What changes: instead of reconciling payments manually each month, every transaction gets a documented audit trail automatically. SLA on payment issue resolution is under 48 hours. For a CFO doing investor diligence, that documentation stack is the difference between a clean answer and three weeks of manual reconstruction. Can we schedule a 20-minute review of your specific setup?
[No reply — the prospect went silent. Try a follow-up.]
What changes: instead of reconciling payments manually each month, every transaction gets a documented audit trail automatically. SLA on payment issue resolution is under 48 hours.
[No reply — the prospect went silent. Try a follow-up.]
Marina, I will send you a one-pager: flow, SLA, and what the audit trail looks like before diligence. If it speaks to your situation, a 20-minute call this week — does that work?
[No reply — the prospect went silent. Try a follow-up.]
